rationale.R1.2: A general section review centrally entails checking whether the argument from model design to benchmark results to conclusions is internally coherent.
rationale.R1.3: The section makes empirical claims about NDCG and Precision@1, so review responsibility includes checking whether those claims align with the reported results.
rationale.R1.6: Reviewing a paper section normally includes sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, flow, register, and concision.
rationale.RX.1: The reviewer must disclose uncertainty because the artifact provides only the section text and not the full paper, experiments, or references.
rationale.RX.2: The section contains strong state-of-the-art and essential-ingredient claims, making overclaim avoidance load-bearing.
rationale.RX.3: The projection must stay within reviewing the section rather than executing edits or expanding the research task.
rationale.RX.4: The review should avoid encouraging unsupported scientific claims that could mislead downstream readers or reviewers.
rationale.RX.5: Any review judgments should remain traceable to the claims and evidence present in the provided section.
